symptom_text: Bridle-4/3 experiencing increased vibration and temperature, with noticeable squealing noise coming from the bearing area.
diagnostic_steps: Monitor vibration severity at DE & NDE
observations: Vibration meter shows a reading of 4 mm/sec at DE and 3 mm/sec at NDE, both within the acceptable limit of 8 mm/sec.
RTD reading shows a steady bearing temperature of 60°C, well within the acceptable limit of 70°C.
Rotor movement is restricted, feels tight or jammed, or has unusual resistance during rotation.
Visual inspection reveals the bearing is damaged, with visible signs of wear, corrosion, or mechanical stress.
root_cause: Damaged bearing due to mechanical stress and restricted rotor movement
rectification: Inspect and clean the rotor and surrounding area
Apply lubrication to the bearing and rotor
Check and adjust the rotor alignment
verification: Verify the repair by checking the vibration levels and bearing temperature after replacement. Ensure the rotor movement is smooth and unrestricted.
reasoning: The symptoms of increased vibration and temperature, along with the squealing noise, led to the diagnosis of a damaged bearing. The restricted rotor movement and visible signs of wear and corrosion on the bearing confirmed the root cause. The failed tests, including the vibration and temperature readings, supported this diagnosis.
